pattern: A positive power of two has exactly one set bit, so clearing the lowest set bit must yield zero; non-positive inputs are never powers of two.
hints: Powers of two in binary are a single 1 followed by zeros, so think about how many bits are set.
Zero and negatives can never qualify; for positives, check that only one bit is on.
n is a power of two iff n > 0 and (n & (n - 1)) == 0, since subtracting one flips the lowest set bit and all trailing zeros.

edge_inputs.description: zero is not a power of two
negative power-of-two magnitude is still not a power of two
largest power of two within 32-bit range
one less than a power of two has many bits set

stress.description: true powers of two sampled across the 32-bit exponent range
stress.note: answer always true; verifies no false negatives on exact powers

stress.description: arbitrary integers including negatives and zero
